mission.title: oauth2-proxy: improved audience handling to support client credentials access tokens without aud claims
mission.description:
This PR adds extra options to optionally skip `aud` verification, if `aud` is missing in the bearer token and the possibility to define an  alternative claim to verify against the client id:
- `skip-aud-check-when-missing` (bool)
- `audience-verification-claim` (string)
- `extra-audiences-for-verification` ([]string)

### skip-aud-check-when-missing
If enabled, audience is only verified if the `aud` claim exists in the token. If not, there is no verification of  audience.

This will internally set `SkipClientIDCheck` in the underlying go-oidc library to true.
https://github.com/AOEpeople/oauth2-proxy/blob/69dfbce99a158556010da4d60c1d5864abd86e3a/pkg/validation/options.go#L123

If set, the verification will happen within oauth2-proxy to have more control over the details of token verification:
https://github.com/AOEpeople/oauth2-proxy/blob/69dfbce99a158556010da4d60c1d5864abd86e3a/providers/provider_data.go#L175

### audience-verification-claim
Can be specified along with `skip-aud-check

resolution.summary: @kschu91 @czenker Are you using both client credentials and user ID Tokens? Seems we need to do some work on this PR to allow them both to co-exist
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
